original: 100 volunteers in Bhutan set a world record for tree planting - 49,672 in 1 hour
Chinese spacecraft Chang'e-6 successfully lands on the far side of the Moon on a mission to analyse and collect rock samples
Europe launches its first voyage to another planet, Mars. The European Space Agency's Mars Express probe launches from the Baikonur space centre in Kazakhstan.
GymnastSimone Biles(27) wins her record 9th US Championship in Fort Worth, Texas
Harriet Tubmanleads Union guerrillas into Maryland, freeing slaves
"Wonder Woman" directed by Patty Jenkins released, earns over $100 million in North American in its opening weekend - domestic record for a female director
US Surveyor 1 lands in Oceanus Procellarum; 1st lunar soft-landing
P. T. Barnum& his circus begin 1st tour of US
Fisherman George W. Perry catches the world record largemouth bass, weighing in at 22 pounds, 4 ounces, at Lake Montgomery, Georgia
Norton Juster, American architect and children's author (The Phantom Tollbooth; The Dot and The Line), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2021)[1][2]
14-year-old Scott Isaacs spells spoliator to win 1989 Spelling Bee
"Turtle Power" by Partners In Kryme hits #13
Mariners draftKen Griffey Jr.#1
Pontiac's Rebellion: At what is now Mackinaw City, Michigan, Chippewas capture Fort Michilimackinac by diverting the garrison's attention with a game of lacrosse, then chasing a ball into the fort
Italian engineer and inventorGuglielmo Marconiapplies for the first ever patent for a system of wireless telegraphy in the United Kingdom

Capitol Records releases The Beatles' album "Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" in the US; it is their first album with identical track listings in US and UK, and goes to number one for 15 weeks in US, 22 weeks in UK
Donati Comet 1st seen named after its discoverer
Pete Conrad, American US Navy pilot and NASA astronaut (Gemini 5, Gemini 11, Apollo 12, Skylab 2), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1999)
Coronation ofQueen Elizabeth IIin Westminster Abbey, London, England
Queen Elizabeth IImarks her Platinum Jubilee with four days of celebrations starting with a military parade at Buckingham Palace
Future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerBabe Ruthannounces his retirement as a player at 40 years of age
Sergio Agüero, Argentine soccer striker (96 caps; Manchester City; leading overseas goal scorer in EPL history), born in Buenos Aires, Argentina
Alexander Graham Bellmakes first sound transmission
FDRauthorizes the first swimming pool built inside the White House
Red Sox starTed Williamsenlists as a US Navy aviator
1st roundtrip flight over English Channel (C S Rolls, England)
Awkwafina [Nora Lum], American rapper ("Yellow Ranger") and actress (The Farewell; Crazy Rich Asians; Ocean's 8), born in New York City
Marvin Hamlisch, American EGOT (Emmy; Grammy; Oscar; and Tony Awards winner) and Pulitzer  Prize-winning  composer and conductor (The Sting; A Chorus Line), born in New York City (d. 2012)

Larry Robinson, Canadian Hockey HOF defenceman (Stanley Cup x 6 Montreal Canadiens; James Norris Memorial Trophy 1977, 80; Conn Smythe Trophy 1978) and coach (Stanley Cup 2000 NJ Devils; LA Kings), born in Winchester, Ontario
First patent for adrenaline (epinephrine) granted to Japanese-American chemist Jokichi Takamine
First recorded snowfall in London in June
1st night baseball under lights, Fort Wayne Indiana
Chicago's elevated train system, the "L", opens to traffic
Construction begins on Clay St (San Francisco) for world's 1st cable railroad
1980 movie "Attack of the Killer Tomatoes" released in Germany
61st National Spell Bee: Rageshree Ramachandran wins spelling elegiacal
Gilbert Baker, American gay activist who designed the rainbow flag, born in Chanute, Kansas (d. 2017)
B-Real [Louis Freese], American rapper (Cypress Hill), born in Los Angeles, California
Heather Couper, British astronomer and presenter (The Sky at Night), born in Wallesey, England (d. 2020)
NASA launches space vehicle S-198
Ruth Atkinson, American cartoonist and pioneering female comic book writer and artist (Millie the Model in Marvel Comics), born in Toronto, Ontario (d. 1997)
"Dead Poets Society", directed by Peter Weir and starringRobin Williamsand Ethan Hawke, premieres in America
Jerry Mathers, American actor (Leave It To Beaver - "Theodore 'Beaver' Cleaver"; The Trouble with Harry), born in Sioux City, Iowa
Amber Marshall, Canadian actress (Heartland), born in London, Ontario
Justin Long, American actor (Jeepers Creepers; Alvin and the Chipmunks), born in Fairfield, Connecticut
Fredrik Backman, Swedish author (A Man Called Ove; Beartown), born in Söderort, Sweden
Dana Carvey, American comedian and actor (SNL - "George Bush"; Wayne's World - "Garth"), born in Missoula, Montana
Zachary Quinto, American actor (Star Trek), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
Morena Baccarin, Brazilian-American actress (Serenity; Deadpool; Homeland), born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
Michael Steele [Susan Thomas], American rock bassist (Bangles - "Eternal Flame"; "Walk Like an Egyptian"), born in Pasadena, California
Steve Waugh, Australian cricket all-rounder, captain (168 Tests, 10,927 runs @ 51.06, 32 x 100, HS 200, 92 wickets; NSWCA, Somerset CCC, Kent CCC), born in Sydney, Australia
Robert Morris Page, American physicist who helped develop and refine radar technology, born in Saint Paul, Minnesota (d. 1992)[1]
Maurice Olley of General Motors begins designing the Corvette
Glenn Curtiss flies his Langley Aerodrome
99th Pursuit Squadron flies 1st combat mission (over Italy)
Flight readiness firing of Discovery's main engines
"Welcome To Fun Zone" hosted by Dr Demento airs on NBC-TV
Earl Young, American session and touring drummer, considered the father of the disco beat (The Trammps; MFSB), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
William Guest, American soul singer (Gladys Knight & the Pips - "Midnight Train to Georgia", "I Heard it Through the Grapevine"), born in Atlanta, Georgia (d. 2015)[1]
Ilaiyaraaja [R. Gnanathesikan], Indian film and concert composer (Thiruvasagam in Symphony), born in Pannaipuram, Tamil Nadu, British Raj
